headline: Marvell Stock Pops. Why Wall Street Sees Upside.
summary: While chip makers Intel and Micron have been getting all the love in recent days,  Marvell  got a nice bounce after analysts at BofA and Goldman Sachs raised their price targets for it.  Marvell shares closed up 8.2% to $177.95 on Wednesday, for a new record high.  The stock was up a further 4.2% to $185.40 in premarket trading Thursday.

content.title: Marvell Stock Pops. Why Wall Street Sees Upside.
content.summary: While chip makers Intel and Micron have been getting all the love in recent days,  Marvell  got a nice bounce after analysts at BofA and Goldman Sachs raised their price targets for it.  Marvell shares closed up 8.2% to $177.95 on Wednesday, for a new record high.  The stock was up a further 4.2% to $185.40 in premarket trading Thursday.

headline: Denmark Data Center Market Investment Analysis Report 2026-2031: $3.19 Bn Growth Opportunities Across 33 Existing, 11 Upcoming Facilities, and 14+ Locations
summary: Denmark's data center market is poised to hit USD 3.19 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 8.52% from 2025 to 2031. Key highlights include approximately 33 operational colocation centers, mainly Tier III certified, with Copenhagen as the primary hub hosting 21 centers. Data center construction costs in Denmark range between $10-$12 million per MW, making Denmark a competitive choice compared to other Nordics. The Danish government's tax incentives attract global players like Prime Data Centers

headline: UK opens antitrust probe into Microsoft's business software
summary: Britain launched an antitrust investigation into Microsoft's dominance in business software that could ‌lead to targeted action if the U.S. company ‌is found to have "strategic market status" in the sector.  It ‌would also look ⁠at how AI competitors were able to integrate with Microsoft's business software, it said.  An SMS ⁠designation would also allow the CMA to intervene in the cloud market, where it has previously found Microsoft's use of ​software licensing ​could be reducing competition ​in cloud services.

content.title: UK opens antitrust probe into Microsoft's business software
content.summary: Britain launched an antitrust investigation into Microsoft's dominance in business software that could ‌lead to targeted action if the U.S. company ‌is found to have "strategic market status" in the sector.  It ‌would also look ⁠at how AI competitors were able to integrate with Microsoft's business software, it said.  An SMS ⁠designation would also allow the CMA to intervene in the cloud market, where it has previously found Microsoft's use of ​software licensing ​could be reducing competition ​in cloud services.

headline: Elon Musk's court battle against OpenAI enters homestretch
summary: A trial that may shape the future of OpenAI enters its final stages on Thursday, as lawyers for Elon Musk try to convince a jury to hold the ChatGPT maker's leaders responsible for transforming the ‌nonprofit into a vehicle to enrich themselves.  Closing arguments are scheduled in the Oakland, California, federal court in Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, ‌Sam Altman.  Musk is suing OpenAI and Altman for breach of charitable trust and unjust enrichment, accusing them of "stealing a charity" by straying from OpenAI's founding mission to build safe AI that would benefit ​humanity.

content.title: Elon Musk's court battle against OpenAI enters homestretch
content.summary: A trial that may shape the future of OpenAI enters its final stages on Thursday, as lawyers for Elon Musk try to convince a jury to hold the ChatGPT maker's leaders responsible for transforming the ‌nonprofit into a vehicle to enrich themselves.  Closing arguments are scheduled in the Oakland, California, federal court in Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, ‌Sam Altman.  Musk is suing OpenAI and Altman for breach of charitable trust and unjust enrichment, accusing them of "stealing a charity" by straying from OpenAI's founding mission to build safe AI that would benefit ​humanity.

headline: Why The Market Is Re-Rating Google Stock
summary: Google (GOOGL) stock currently trades near $402. Based on an expected 2026 EPS of $14.22, the stock carries a forward P/E multiple of 28x. This multiple represents a 35% premium over its five-year historical average forward P/E of 21x. Comparatively, Google stock sits between Microsoft (MSFT) at 24x and Amazon (AMZN) at 31x, while remaining more expensive than Meta Platforms (META) at 19x. headline: Microsoft products to be investigated by UK competition regulator
summary: The competition regulator has launched an investigation into Microsoft over software used by hundreds of thousands of UK businesses and public sector organisations.  The CMA will assess where bundling of Microsoft products limits in interoperability, the ability of computer systems to work together and exchange information, and if default settings prevent users from switching to alternatives.  It had heard that UK customers may not always be able to effectively combine software from Microsoft with other providers, limiting their access to the best products at the lowest prices.

content.title: Microsoft products to be investigated by UK competition regulator
content.summary: The competition regulator has launched an investigation into Microsoft over software used by hundreds of thousands of UK businesses and public sector organisations.  The CMA will assess where bundling of Microsoft products limits in interoperability, the ability of computer systems to work together and exchange information, and if default settings prevent users from switching to alternatives.  It had heard that UK customers may not always be able to effectively combine software from Microsoft with other providers, limiting their access to the best products at the lowest prices.
